data.title: Bitcoin Reserve Legislation Gains Momentum Across U.S. States
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, as of February 20, data from SoSovalue indicates that twenty state-level jurisdictions in the United States are initiating legislative processes related to Bitcoin reserve strategies. Fifteen of these states have seen their bills formally accepted by legislative houses and are now under committee review, marking a regional legislative wave for Bitcoin reserve laws.

In Utah, for instance, Bill HB0230 proposes allowing four public funds—the State Disaster Recovery Restricted Account, the General Fund Budget Reserve Account, the Income Tax Fund Budget Reserve Account, and the Medicaid Growth Reduction and Budget Stabilization Account—to invest up to 5% of their total assets in Bitcoin. Based on Utah's 2024 financial report, this could translate to a potential purchasing power exceeding $70 million.

Analysts from SoSovalue suggest that the strategic Bitcoin reserve legislation represents not only a breakthrough for Bitcoin within the traditional financial system but also a historic advancement. As more state governments explore similar strategies, the trend of public funds holding Bitcoin may redefine the global market's perception of Bitcoin as an asset. It is increasingly being seen not just as digital gold but as a component of national and institutional asset allocation, further solidifying its position in the global financial system.

data.title: A certain address transferred 100 million DOGE to Binance, equivalent to about 25.42 million US dollars
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Whale Alert monitoring, about 2 minutes ago, a certain address transferred 100 million DOGE to Binance, equivalent to approximately 25.42 million US dollars.

data.title: OdinSwap And Valhalla To Cease Operations By February 2025
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the Bitcoin network's decentralized exchange platform OdinSwap and the launch platform Valhalla have announced plans to gradually cease operations. The platforms will continue to operate until February 28, 2025, ensuring that all user funds remain secure. Users are advised to withdraw their funds by this date. After February 28, any unclaimed balances will be refunded manually, subject to applicable Bitcoin network fees. The refund process may take up to a month to complete and will only apply to balances exceeding the network fees.

During this transition period, OdinSwap remains open to strategic partnerships and invites potential partners interested in integrating the platform to reach out.

Previously, BlockBeats reported that as of February 15, the seven-day moving average of Bitcoin network transactions had decreased to 330,000, nearing a 12-month low. This marks a significant 55% drop from the historical peak of 730,000 transactions on the network. Additionally, the fees generated by Runes over the past 30 days have fallen to less than $20,000, a stark contrast to the $60 million generated on its launch day.

data.title: BitMart will launch KAITO/USDT contracts, supporting 50x leverage
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News BitMart will launch the perpetual contract of the token KAITO (KAITO) at 21:00 (UTC+8) on February 20, 2025, and open the KAITO/USDT trading pair with a maximum leverage of 50 times.
Kaito AI is building an InfoFi network driven by artificial intelligence, enabling the market to more efficiently reallocate attention and capital while rewarding all participants.

data.title: Story Ecosystem One-Stop IPFi Lending Platform Unleash Currently IP Lending Annualized 259.66%

data.title: Vitalik: Blockchain cannot have no bottom line on moral issues, the foundation will make changes to promote development
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: In a text AMA held on the Tako platform, Vitalik answered the question "Have you ever been frustrated and disappointed with EF, the community, and the industry in the past year?" He said: Of course. Perhaps the most disappointing thing for me recently is that some people say that Ethereum is not good and intolerant because we do not respect the "casinos" on the blockchain enough, and other chains are happy to accept any application, so they are better. If the blockchain community has this kind of "reverse Tiangang" moral problem, I will have no interest in participating in the blockchain. But I found an interesting point: on the Internet, many people will say those words, but when I chat with the community in person, everyone's values ​​are still the same as before, so I feel that I have a responsibility to this community and cannot abandon them. Ethereum needs to work together to create the world we want to see. This will require some changes, such as the foundation may not be too neutral at the application layer, and it needs to deliberately support some things, but the project must be worth it for us to do so.
